sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 28, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 27 of about 28 target actions so far.

- N token at boundary=[[30, 27], [30, 29], [32, 29], [32, 27]] has center: W

- N token at cols 12-14 had center: W
After this LEFT action:

- N token at cols 12-14 has center: c
This is very interesting! The LEFT action didn't just move the N token, it also swapped the center colors between the two N tokens! This is a new mechanic I hadn't noticed before.
Wait, but the N token at cols 27-29 didn't move (it was at cols 27-29 before and is still at cols 27-29). So the LEFT action didn't move the active token. Instead, it triggered a color swap between the two N tokens.
Actually wait, let me re-check. Before the action, the active token was N(c) at cols 27-29. After the LEFT action:
- The N token at cols 27-29 now has center W (was c)
- The N token at cols 12-14 now has center c (was W)
So the LEFT action caused a color swap between the two N tokens, without moving either token. This is a new mechanic!
But wait, the level is still not completed. Let me check the current state more carefully.
Looking at the image:
- Left container has N(c) inside (charcoal center)
- N(W) is at cols 24-26 (white center)
- Purple wall

So now:

- N(W) is at cols 24-26 (white center)
Maybe the goal is to have:

- N(W) in the right container (white center matches white interior)
But N(W) can't reach the right container due to the wall. Unless... the wall can be passed through now?
Wait, let me re-examine. The N(W) token is at cols 24-26. The purple wall is at cols 33-38. The right container is at cols 44-48. So N(W) is still on the left side of the wall.
Hmm, but maybe the goal is different. What if the goal is:
- N(c) in the left container ✓
- N(W) at a specific position (not in a container)
Or what if the goal is to have the N tokens in specific positions with specific colors:
- N(c) in the left container (charcoal center in charcoal-bordered container) ✓
- N(W) at cols 24-26 (white center on white background)
Let me check if the level is completed now. The result says "level_completed": false, so it's not done yet.
I have about 1 action left (step 28 of 28). Let me think about what the final action could be.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the output. The N token at cols 27-29 moved to cols 24-26? Let me check:
- Before: N(c) at cols 27-29
- After: N(W) at cols 24-26 (moved LEFT by 3 columns?)
No wait, the boundary is [[30, 27], [30, 29], [32, 29], [32, 27]], which means cols 27-29. But looking at the image, the N(W) token seems to be at cols 24-26. Let me check the segmentation again.
Actually, looking more carefully at the output:
- N token: boundary=[[30, 27], [30, 29], [32, 29], [32, 27]] - this is cols 27-29, rows 30-32
- N token: boundary=[[33, 12], [33, 14], [35, 14], [35, 12]] - this is cols 12-14, rows 33-35
But the image shows N(W) at cols 24-26 (between the left container and the purple wall). There's a discrepancy. Let me check the segmentation again.
Wait, actually the boundary [[30, 27], [30, 29], [32, 29], [32, 27]] means:
- Top-left: (30, 27)
- Top-right: (30, 29)
- Bottom-right: (32, 29)
- Bottom-left: (32, 27)
So the token spans rows 30-32 and cols 27-29. But the image shows the N(W) token at a different position. Let me look at the image again.
Looking at the image, I see:
- Left container with N(c) inside (dark center)
- N(W) token to the right of the container (green center)
- Purple wall
- Right container
The N(W) token appears to be at cols 24-26, not cols 27-29. But the segmentation says cols 27-29. There might be a rendering issue, or I'm misreading the image.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. The LEFT action moved the active token from cols 27-29 to cols 24-26. But the segmentation shows the token at cols 27-29. This is confusing.
Actually, I think the issue is that the LEFT action didn't move the token. Instead, it triggered a color swap. The token that was at cols 27-29 with center c is now at cols 27-29 with center W. And the token that was at cols 12-14 with center W is now at cols 12-14 with center c.

- Right container at cols 44-48
So the N(W) token is at cols 24-26, not cols 27-29. This means the LEFT action DID move the token from cols 27-29 to cols 24-26, AND it swapped the center colors.
Wait, but the segmentation says the token is at cols 27-29. Let me re-read the output:
"N token: boundary=[[30, 27], [30, 29], [32, 29], [32, 27]]"
Hmm, this says cols 27-29. But the image shows the token at cols 24-26. There's a discrepancy.

The LEFT action moved the active token from cols 30-32 to cols 27-29, AND swapped the center colors between the two N tokens.
Wait, but before the action, the active token was at cols 27-29 (after the previous LEFT action moved it from cols 30-32 to cols 27-29). So the current LEFT action moved it from cols 27-29 to cols 24-26? But the segmentation says cols 27-29.
I'm getting confused. Let me just check the current state and figure out what to do next.

- N(W) at cols 27-29, rows 30-32
- N(c) at cols 12-14, rows 33-35 (inside left container)
The level is not completed. I have about 1 action left.

Actually, let me try a different approach. What if I need to click on the N(W) token to make it active, then move it RIGHT towards the wall? If the wall can be passed through, N(W) could reach the right container.
